metadata.title: Why 48 Iron Atoms Make Quantum Ripples Nobody Placed There
metadata.note: Clean Liam rebuild. Correctly describes Cu(111) surface-state LDOS and Fe-adatom scattering; avoids false hard-wall and 1D wavelength claims.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. In 1993, IBM researchers arranged forty-eight iron atoms into a circle of

beats.prompt: The copper one-one-one surface supports electrons that move approximately as a two-dimensional wave gas along the surface.
beats.label: The copper one-one-one surface supports electrons that move approximately as a two-dimensional wave

beats.prompt: Each iron adatom is a strong scatterer. Forty-eight of them form a circular barrier, but not an ideal impenetrable wall.
beats.label: Each iron adatom is a strong scatterer. Forty-eight of them form a circular barrier, but not an idea

beats.prompt: A surface electron wave travels outward, scatters from the ring, and returns. The outgoing and returning amplitudes add or cancel according to phase.
beats.label: A surface electron wave travels outward, scatters from the ring, and returns. The outgoing and retur

beats.prompt: Constructive interference produces bright probability-amplitude bands. Destructive interference produces dark nodes between them.
beats.label: Constructive interference produces bright probability-amplitude bands. Destructive interference prod

beats.prompt: Only wave patterns compatible with the circular confinement build up strongly. The boundary turns a continuum into a set of resonant spatial modes.

beats.prompt: For rotationally symmetric modes, the maxima and nodes are circles. Other allowed modes can carry angular lobes instead of perfect rings.

beats.prompt: So the famous image is not a photograph of forty-eight electrons orbiting. It is a spatial map of energy-resolved surface-state interference.

Forty-eight atoms built the boundary. Interference drew the rings.
